metadata.title:
WOMAN


THROWN

AT DIVISION AND
EMMA STEPHENS ASKS FIF-
TEEN THOUSAND DOLLARS
DAMAGES.

pages.raw_text:
The crossing at Sprague avenue and
Division street is the cause of another
suit against the Northern Pacific rail-
road company. Emma Stephens has
filed an action to recover SIS, l20 for
injuries received there.


The complaint alleges that on Nov
ember 2, 1908, Mrs. Stephens was
driving with her daughter. They APP
preached the crossing and were halted
by the Waterman. After train had
passed she was told to proceed. While
yet on the tracks light engine passed
and scared her horse, causing it to run
away. Mrs. Stephens was thrown out
of the carriage on her head and back.


As a result she has lost her hearing
in one ear, her mind and memory have
been affected, and her back has caused
her SO much pain that she has been
unable to carry on her business of dress
making. She asks the sum of $40 for
her carriage, $50 for her doctor bill,
and $500 for the injuries she has
suffered
